today_in_history_posts.title: Inquiry: Transaction Tiering
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Martin Stolze inquires about consensus on transaction tiering and its current protocol issues.
- Stolze explores options under Lessig's Cyberspace concepts: Law, Architecture, Market, for fair user transactions.
- He suggests allowing BSAs to compete for business on factors beyond price, highlighting integrity in Bitcoin's future.

today_in_history_posts.title: [RFC] Lightning payment format
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The post outlines a draft for lightning payment requests, hosted on GitHub.
- It details an optimization using an 8-byte chanid for shorter references.
- The author invites critiques and substantial feedback on their proposal.

today_in_history_posts.title: Deflationary money is a Good Thing
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Investment strategies are divided into passive and active types, each with distinct characteristics.
- Passive investment's hands-off approach can lead to societal issues like asset underutilization and principal/agent problems.
- The article suggests central banks using deflationary currencies like Bitcoin could stabilize economies, despite implementation challenges.
